3. Uses `df` for disk usage

Let me make this portable and robust.

content.text: I'll create this bash script directly — it's a

arguments.content:
#!/bin/bash
# sysinfo.sh - System information report
# Compatible with Linux and macOS

set -e

echo "=== System Information ==="
echo ""

# CPU Model
echo "CPU Model:"
if command -v sysctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # macOS and some Linux systems
    if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
        sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown Processor"
    else
        grep "model name" /proc/cpuinfo 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d: -f2 | sed 's/^ //' || \
            sysctl -n hw.model 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown Processor"
    fi
else
    echo "Unknown Processor"
fi
echo ""

# Total RAM
echo "Total RAM:"
if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
    # macOS
    RAM_BYTES=$(sysctl -n hw.memsize 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    RAM_GB=$((RAM_BYTES / 1024 / 1024 / 1024))
    echo "${RAM_GB} GB"
else
    # Linux
    if [[ -r /proc/meminfo ]]; then
        RAM_KB=$(grep MemTotal /proc/meminfo | awk '{print $2}')
        RAM_GB=$((RAM_KB / 1024 / 1024))
        echo "${RAM_GB} GB"
    else
        echo "Unable to determine"
    fi
fi
echo ""

# Disk Usage
echo "Disk Usage:"
df -h / 2>/dev/null | tail -1 || echo "Unable to get disk usage"
echo ""

echo "=== End of Report ==="
